name: Zazaki
notes: Zazaki (Dimli / Kirmanjki), a Northwestern Iranian language of eastern Anatolia. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Zazaki is written in a Latin alphabet derived from the Kurdish Hawar alphabet, and also (historically and in some communities) in an Arabic-derived script; no authoritative letter-to-phoneme table for the Zazaki Latin alphabet could be verified, and the phoneme inventory includes pharyngealised consonants and a central-vowel contrast whose orthographic representation is not settled. Latin-script input is assumed for any future map; the Arabic-script tradition (an abjad) is out of scope. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode diml1238), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode diml1238.

ancestors.notes: Iranian — the classification node this language sits under.
